footer.description: A behavioral reading layer to understand when digital flows are still working, but have already started losing consistency.
footer.tagline: Ohrly does not eliminate incidents. It eliminates the silence before them.

hero.titleStart: Discover where your digital flows start getting worse
hero.description: Analyze checkout, payment, delivery, support or onboarding and receive a clear reading of what changed, how long ago it changed and where your operation may be losing consistency.
hero.note: Start with 1 critical flow. No integration required.

readingExample.title: Payment flow
readingExample.description: The payment flow started losing consistency 4 days ago. Approval time is 2.8x above the expected baseline for Pix mobile on business days. The behavior exceeded the natural recovery cycle and started propagating to new payment signals.
readingExample.timelineNote: The flow moved from normal variation into attention and remains in sustained degradation for 4 days.

howItWorks.step1Description: Checkout, payment, delivery, support, onboarding or another important flow.
howItWorks.step2Description: You can start with a spreadsheet, export or existing events.
howItWorks.step3Description: Ohrly shows where the flow moved away from expected behavior, how long ago and what deserves attention.

compactReport.title: From a raw CSV to a decision reading.
compactReport.description: We used a synthetic retention dataset for a boleto flow to simulate how Ohrly interprets a critical flow: first it understands the file, then applies a behavioral contract and finally generates an operational reading.
compactReport.inputTitle: Support and retention dataset for boleto
compactReport.ohrlyRole: Ohrly does not decide for the team. It organizes the investigation: shows where pressure appeared, which signals are more reliable and which hypotheses deserve priority in the next conversation.

mainEpisode.title: Main episode
mainEpisode.description: 1.047 exposed events expostos, RPI 68.93 and 60% of consumed decision window.

compactReport.finalReadingDescription: The degradation was not isolated: it appeared in retention, handoff, complaint, boleto failure, resolution and human time.
compactReport.whatItMeans: Given this context, what does it mean?
compactReport.meaningTitle: The flow did not show only an isolated fluctuation. It accumulated operational pressure across multiple signals, with propagation and high statistical confidence.
compactReport.meaningDescription: In a call, this helps the team move from “I think it got worse” to an evidence-based conversation: what changed, how long ago, where it concentrated and how many events went through degradation periods.

fileColumns.label: File columns
fileColumns.description: raw fields available in the CSV

mappedFields.label: Mapped fields
mappedFields.description: entity, context, metrics and impact signals

configuredMetrics.label: Configured metrics
configuredMetrics.description: retention, resolution, failure, handoff, complaint and time metrics

baseline.label: Baseline
baseline.description: contextual rolling baseline with hierarchical fallback

highPressureEvents.label: Events in high pressure
highPressureEvents.description: events in high or critical pressure episodes

compareOperationalChanges.title: Compare with operational changes
compareOperationalChanges.description: Cross-check the highest-pressure periods with deployments, rule changes, campaigns or support changes.

investigateMetricPropagation.title: Investigate propagation across metrics
investigateMetricPropagation.description: Check whether the retention drop appeared together with handoff, complaints, boleto failures or increased human handling time.

prioritizeHighConfidenceSignals.title: Prioritize high-confidence signals
prioritizeHighConfidenceSignals.description: Start with episodes with higher volume and statistical confidence before investigating weaker signals.

trackRecurrence.title: Track recurrence
trackRecurrence.description: Compare the highlighted episodes with previous occurrences to identify recurring degradation families.

sharedReading.title: You already have data. What is missing is a shared reading.
sharedReading.description: These signals often belong to the same problem, but appear separately. Ohrly connects them into a simple answer:
sharedReading.question: Is this flow still healthy or has it started losing consistency?

dashboardComparison.title: Ohrly looks at the flow, not only the system
dashboardComparison.description: The system can remain up, with no critical error and within SLA. Even so, the behavior of the flow may already have started getting worse.
dashboardComparison.ohrlyQuestion: Is the flow still working well?
dashboardComparison.flowDegraded: The flow is still up, but has lost consistency for 4 days.
dashboardComparison.bottomDescription: The difference is not seeing more charts. It is understanding when a flow is still working, but has already started generating more waiting time, retries, abandonment, fallback, human support and rework.

readingItems.noiseOrDegradation: Whether it looks like noise or degradation
readingItems.exposure: Which operation or value is exposed

personas.title: Who it is for
personas.ecommerceDescription: Understand where the purchase funnel loses consistency before revenue is impacted.
personas.productDescription: See the real behavior of the flows that sustain the user experience.
personas.engineeringDescription: Prioritize problems that are already relevant in behavior, not only in the system.
personas.operationsDescription: Anticipate queues, delays and rework before they become explicit operational cost.

cta.title: Want to discover whether one of your flows has already started getting worse?
cta.description: Run a simple pilot with 1 critical flow.
cta.note: You do not need to change your architecture to get started.

contact.title: Should we apply an Ohrly reading to one of your real flows?
contact.description: Tell us which flow you want to understand. From there, we can evaluate whether it makes sense to run a simple pilot with historical data to identify loss of consistency, recovery pressure and affected contexts.

bullets.two: Can start with an export or CSV.
bullets.three: Focused on operational reading, not a generic dashboard.

form.title: Request a conversation
form.description: Tell us a little about the flow you would like to analyze. The idea is to start simple: one critical flow, historical data and a behavioral reading.
form.name: Name
form.flowType: Which flow would you like to analyze?
form.message: What would you like to understand about this flow?
form.messagePlaceholder: E.g. I want to understand whether checkout conversion started dropping after the latest changes, or whether the payment flow is getting slower in some contexts.
form.privacy: By submitting, you share this information so we can evaluate whether it makes sense to run an initial reading of your flow.

historicalData.title: Start with historical data
historicalData.description: A spreadsheet, export or simple dataset may already be enough for a first diagnosis.

criticalFlow.title: Focus on one critical flow
criticalFlow.description: Checkout, payment, delivery, support, onboarding or another flow with real impact.

noRootCausePromise.title: No root-cause promise
noRootCausePromise.description: The reading shows where behavior lost consistency and helps qualify the investigation.

noIntegration.title: No integration at first
noIntegration.description: The pilot can start without changing your operation architecture.
